result:
All changes are complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Changes Made

### 1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/11-arrays.xml`
Added a new string resource `gesture_reschedule` with value "Reschedule" — this is the label that appears in the gesture/controller button mapping settings.

### 2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`
Added `RESCHEDULE_CARD(R.string.gesture_reschedule)` as a new enum value at the end of the `ViewerCommand` enum. This registers "Reschedule" as an available command that users can map to gestures, keyboard shortcuts, or controller buttons.

### 3. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt`
Added handling for `ViewerCommand.RESCHEDULE_CARD` in the `executeCommand()` method. When triggered, it calls the existing `showRescheduleCardDialog()` method, which displays the dialog for rescheduling the current card.

### 4. `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommandTest.kt`
Updated the `preference_keys_are_not_changed` test to include `binding_RESCHEDULE_CARD` in the expected list. The test comment explicitly states "Adds are acceptable."

### How it works
- Users can now assign the "Reschedule" command to any gesture (swipe, tap), keyboard shortcut, or game controller button through the existing settings UI
- When activated, it opens the reschedule dialog directly — a single gesture/button press instead of multiple taps through menus
- No default keybinding is assigned (same as most other commands like `SHOW_ANSWER`, `DELETE`, etc.), so it won't interfere with existing configurations
- The command follows the exact same pattern as other existing commands like `BURY_CARD`, `UNDO`, `ADD_NOTE`, etc.
